text: WardrobeOS now opens with a calmer, cleaner homepage. The product is organized around real wardrobe decisions instead of a technical dashboard.
I sign in with a fresh dummy account, using the same email code flow a beta user would see.
The Today screen starts with one recommendation, one reason, and the next best action. The health rings make the closet status easy to scan.
Next I add a single item. The image uploads, the item autosaves to the server, and metadata can be reviewed before saving.
Bulk upload still works for multiple items. The workflow keeps the review step, but the lighter design makes it easier to follow.
Closet is now a cleaner catalog. Search and the most useful filters are up front, with advanced filters tucked away until needed.
Style keeps the outfit engine, scores, save actions, wear tracking, and GPT try-on handoff, but presents them as visual outfit cards.
Plan uses Robinhood-style decision clarity only where it helps: this buy-next card shows unlocks, confidence, gaps, and owned pieces it pairs with.
The packing planner is still here. It builds a compact trip capsule from owned pieces while respecting weather, laundry, and shoe limits.
Insights keeps the deeper analysis: occasion coverage, duplicates, and underused items.
GPT Stylist remains available for closet-aware prompts and visual try-on workflows, now as an advanced styling tool.
Reliability still protects the upload workflow with diagnostics and recovery actions.
Back on Today, the cleaned up app feels more like a daily wardrobe companion while preserving the full feature set.
